name: Ida Plain
description: Iðavöllr (Ida Plain or Field) is a plain located in Asgard, the realm of the Æsir gods in Norse mythology. It holds immense significance as a central gathering place and a symbol of renewal and rebirth after Ragnarok, the twilight of the gods. Following the destruction of the old world, the surviving gods, including Baldr and Höðr who return from Hel, gather on Ida Plain. Here, they rebuild Asgard, reminisce about the past, and find the golden game pieces they had once used before the cataclysmic events. This act symbolizes the restoration of order, wisdom, and the continuation of divine presence. The plain is described as lush and fertile, signifying a new beginning and the promise of a prosperous future. Iðavöllr is also associated with craftsmanship and creation. It is mentioned that the dwarves, skilled artisans, built their forges and workshops on this plain, further emphasizing its importance as a center of activity and innovation. The resilience and continuity embodied by Iðavöllr offer a powerful message of hope and the cyclical nature of life, death, and rebirth within the Norse worldview. The plain represents not just a physical location, but also a spiritual and symbolic space where the gods reaffirm their commitment to the world and its future. Its association with peace, memory, and the rebuilding of civilization makes it a vital element in the Norse cosmological landscape.
subtitle: Plain of Rebirth and Gathering

keyMyths.title: Rebuilding After Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: After Ragnarok, the surviving gods gather on Ida Plain to rebuild Asgard and find the golden game pieces, symbolizing a new beginning.

keyMyths.title: The Golden Game Pieces
keyMyths.description: The discovery of the golden game pieces on Ida Plain reminds the gods of their past and reinforces their commitment to the future.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson
sources.description: A key source for Norse mythology, including descriptions of Asgard and Ragnarok.
